message:
[review-fix] is_k_regular: empty graph is vacuously k-regular for any k

NetworkX implements ``is_k_regular`` as ``all(d == k for n, d in
degrees())`` — on an empty graph the iterator is empty, so ``all()``
returns True regardless of ``k``, including negative ``k``. The
``if k < 0 { return Ok(false); }`` shortcut introduced in b38bcea
short-circuited that case and diverged from upstream:

    fnx.is_k_regular(fnx.Graph(), -1)  # False — wrong
    nx.is_k_regular(nx.Graph(),  -1)   # True

Return True for an empty graph and False otherwise when ``k < 0``,
which matches the NetworkX semantics exactly. Non-empty graphs still
return False for negative ``k`` (every degree is non-negative, so no
node degree can equal a negative target), so the existing K_4 test
case keeps passing.

Adds ``test_empty_graph_is_vacuously_k_regular_for_any_k`` to lock the
parity in across positive, zero, and negative ``k``.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -154,6 +154,16 @@ def test_negative_k_raises_or_returns_false(self):
             with pytest.raises((ValueError, fnx.NetworkXError)):
                 fnx.is_k_regular(fnx_g, -1)
 
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("k", [-100, -1, 0, 1, 5])
+    def test_empty_graph_is_vacuously_k_regular_for_any_k(self, k):
+        """NetworkX implements ``is_k_regular`` as ``all(d == k for n, d
+        in degrees())`` — on an empty graph the iterator is empty so
+        ``all()`` returns True regardless of ``k``, including negative
+        ``k``. The ``k < 0`` shortcut introduced in b38bcea diverged on
+        that empty-graph edge case; this lock-in test guards parity."""
+        assert nx.is_k_regular(nx.Graph(), k) == fnx.is_k_regular(fnx.Graph(), k)
+        assert fnx.is_k_regular(fnx.Graph(), k) is True
+
     def test_k_larger_than_nodes_returns_false(self):
         """k larger than n-1 should return False."""
         nx_g = nx.complete_graph(5)
